objective: Finish Sage as a complete, hard-cut, bridge-native runtime with a bounded SDK, fresh bridge-native execution services, runtime simplification, and end-to-end cleanup.
scope_summary: Completed the hard-cut bridge-native runtime pass by expanding the bounded SDK, switching Code Mode injection to a manifest-driven contract, simplifying the LangGraph runtime around a single public execution surface, tightening introspection/error surfacing, and removing obsolete multi-tool approval-era tests and cleanup seams.
constraints: Keep runtime_execute_code as the only public tool.
Do not restore legacy tool catalogs, generic dispatch helpers, or fallback adapters.
Preserve the bounded top-level namespace model.

changes.summary: Expanded the bridge-native SDK surface for Discord, moderation, and schedule domains with typed summaries and argument metadata.
Made the bridge contract manifest the single source of truth for child-runner injection and runtime capability introspection.
Removed stale multi-tool and speculative pre-approval runtime branches so approval now only materializes from actual Code Mode effects.
Added provider-free bridge smoke coverage for capabilities, workspace, HTTP, and optional history, and documented Linux CA bundle handling for Node HTTPS.
Deleted obsolete legacy runtime tests that asserted retired multi-tool and pre-approval behavior, and strengthened contract assertions to satisfy the trust audit.

validations.notes: The optional history smoke warns unless SAGE_BRIDGE_SMOKE_CHANNEL_ID is configured.

evidence: The tool audit reports exactly one public tool with zero findings.
Full Vitest passed with 108 files and 691 tests.
Mutation coverage completed at 100 percent for the critical configuration.
decisions: Kept message deletion under moderation instead of reintroducing discord.messages.delete.
Kept the runtime bounded around a manifest-defined SDK instead of reviving generic tool catalogs or public read-batching.
risks: The provider-free smoke intentionally treats history as optional until a smoke channel id is configured, so live history verification still depends on environment setup.
